title: Siege of Jabba's Palace
content:
{{Top|canon=Rescue of Han Solo|unmatched=1}}

}}
{{Quote|There will be no bargain, young Jedi. I shall enjoy watching you die.|[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="RotJ" />|translated=1}}
The '''siege of Jabba's Palace''' was conducted in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] by key members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], including [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] disguised as [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. The objective was to free Han Solo from [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] and rescue him from Jabba the Hutt's possession.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|If I told you half the things I've heard about this Jabba the Hutt, you'd probably short circuit!|C-3PO to R2-D2|<ref name="RotJ" />}}
After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] arrived on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leader [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|frozen]] in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] and handed over to the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to be brought to the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]], as a trophy.<ref name="Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back">{{Film|V}}</ref> [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] returned to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and traveled to [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] old hut in the [[Jundland Wastes/Legends|Jundland Wastes]]. There he discovered [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] training material, from which he learned how to build himself a new green-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire novel">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref> After this, the Rebels set in motion the plan to rescue Solo by first having Lando Calrissian infiltrate Jabba's palace, disguised as the guard [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Tamtel Skreej]].<ref name=":1" />

==The mission==
===C-3PO and R2-D2 Arrive===
{{Quote|I don't think they're going to let us in, Artoo. We better go.|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] to [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], before entering the palace|<ref name="RotJ" />}}
[[File:JabbasTT8L.png|thumb|left|220px|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] confers with Jabba's [[TT-8L/Y7 gatekeeper droid/Legends|gatekeeper droid]].]]
The two [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], arrived at [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] to deliver a message to Jabba. They were greeted and interrogated by a [[TT-8L/Y7 gatekeeper droid/Legends|TT-8L/Y7 gatekeeper droid]] before being granted access. R2-D2 went first, followed reluctantly by C-3PO. Once inside, with the doors closed, they were stopped by Jabba's [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guards and greeted by [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]]. After refusing to give the message to Fortuna, they were brought before Jabba to present the offer.<ref name="RotJ" />

Luke Skywalker's holographic message offered a peaceful resolution in which Jabba would release Solo and in turn would be given the two droids as a show of good faith. Jabba refused, but chose to keep the droids anyway. C-3PO and R2-D2 were brought to [[EV-9D9/Legends|EV-9D9]] and given jobs in Jabba's service. C-3PO became Jabba's translator, and R2-D2 was given the task of serving drinks on the Hutt's [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]].<ref name="RotJ" /> Around the time EV-9D9 had given C-3PO and R2-D2 their orders, one of the Gamorrean guards, [[Jubnuk/Legends|Jubnuk]], attempted to ask the droid inspector about a [[Tusken battle talisman]] that Jabba had misplaced in the palace, but his requests were ignored. He eventually rummaged through the junk, although he eventually resorted to asking the [[B'omarr Order/Legends|B'omarr monks]] about its location due to the noise driving him crazy.<ref name="Pop-Up Palace">''[[Jabba's Palace Pop-up Book]]''</ref>

After the droids were put to work, Fortuna travelled to [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] to run some errands, during which time, he arranged to meet with Skywalker at [[Desilijic Complex/Legends|Jabba's town house]] to hear his offer. Skywalker offered to pay what Solo owed Jabba plus interest, and after using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to deduce Fortuna's concern for his home planet and its people, he assured Fortuna that [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] would be liberated by the Rebellion. Fortuna claimed that he would hand over Solo if Skywalker gave him the money directly, though he did not intend to release Solo alive. Fortuna then returned to the palace and informed Jabba of the offer, but the gangster rejected it and instructed Fortuna not to allow Skywalker to enter the palace.<ref name="Of the Day's Annoyances: Bib Fortuna's Tale" />

===Rescue Attempt and Capture===
{{Quote|We have powerful friends. You're gonna regret this.|Leia Organa to Jabba the Hutt|<ref name="RotJ" />}}
[[File:ChewieCaptive_ST.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Leia, disguised as Boushh, negotiates Chewbacca's bounty]]
Later, Jabba and his court witnessed a performance by the [[Max Rebo Band/Legends|Max Rebo Band]], during which Jabba fed the [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] dancer [[Oola/Legends|Oola]] to his [[Jabba's rancor/Legends|rancor]]. Immediately afterwards, Leia Organa entered the palace disguised as the bounty hunter Boushh, who had by that point been deceased. With her she brought a "captured" Chewbacca for the bounty on his head. In order to ensure that Jabba agreed to the deal, she brought along a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] with the intention of setting it off in the event that Jabba refused the bounty price of no less than 50,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Jabba then laughed it off, impressed with her fearlessness and inventiveness, and they agreed to a reduced price of 35,000 credits. Chewbacca was imprisoned, while Organa remained in the palace.<ref name="RotJ" />

That same night, Organa sneaked into Jabba's throne room and succeeded at freeing Solo from carbonite. As Solo was unable to see his savior due to being blinded by [[Hibernation sickness/Legends|hibernation sickness]], Organa revealed her identity and kissed him. However, Jabba suspected treachery and was waiting to capture the Rebels. After trying to plead with Jabba, Solo was taken to the dungeons and put in a cell with Chewbacca.<ref name="RotJ" /> Meanwhile, Calrissian tried to sneak away with Organa, but Jabba instead demanded that she be brought to him.<ref name="Star Wars: Return of the Jedi novelization">[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]]</ref> Though she tried to threaten the crime lord, Organa recoiled in disgust as Jabba proceeded to lick her face.<ref name="RotJ" /> Jabba then ordered the Max Rebo Band to play music while his dancers stripped her down and dressed her in a [[Huttese slave-girl costume]]. Having been chained to the crime lord's [[Jabba the Hutt's dais/Legends|throne]], Organa was forced to serve Jabba as his personal slave.<ref name="And the Band Played On: The Band's Tale">{{StoryCite|story=And the Band Played On: The Band's Tale|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref>

[[File:JabbaLeia.jpg|thumb|left|230px|After her failed rescue attempt, Leia was enslaved by Jabba.]]
Afterwards, Boba Fett came to see Solo in his prison cell. Though he claimed to have defeated Solo, the smuggler hurled insults and threats at him. Calrissian also checked in on Organa, who assured him that she was alright despite her discomfort. Calrissian decided it would be unsafe to make another escape attempt until Skywalker arrived. They both expressed hope that he would appear soon, as Organa admitted that the thought of having to dance for Jabba made her concerned for her safety.<ref name=":1" />

While the Rebels remained in captivity over the next several days, Jabba's chef [[Porcellus/Legends|Porcellus]] offered to sneak Organa food and prepared meals for Solo to help cure his hibernation sickness.<ref name="Taster's Choice: The Tale of Jabba's Chef">{{StoryCite|story=Taster's Choice: The Tale of Jabba's Chef|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref> One night, Jabba sent Organa to Boba Fett's quarters, but the bounty hunter was not interested in her and instead gave her a sheet to cover herself. When Fett declined Organa's offer to help them escape, the two began to argue about Solo and the Rebellion before Fett eventually grew tired and fell asleep.<ref name="The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett">{{StoryCite|story=The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}}</ref> Meanwhile, Solo and Chewbacca were visited in prison by the B'omarr Monk [[Sai'da]], who offered to do what he could to protect Organa in exchange for Solo telling him stories.<ref name="Star Wars Journal: Hero for Hire">''[[Star Wars Journal: Hero for Hire]]''</ref> 

During this time, several of Jabba's court members were secretly plotting to kill him. The accountant [[Tessek/Legends|Tessek]] had a bomb planted on Jabba's sail barge.<ref name="A Free Quarren in the Palace: Tessek's Tale">{{StoryCite|story=A Free Quarren in the Palace: Tessek's Tale|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref> [[J'Quille/Legends|J'Quille]], who was working as a spy for [[Valarian/Legends|Lady Valarian]], paid a kitchen boy to poison Jabba's food. When this plan fell apart, he came into possession of Organa's thermal detonator,<ref name="Let Us Prey: The Whiphid's Tale">{{StoryCite|story=Let Us Prey: The Whiphid's Tale|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref> though Fortuna stole it from him as part of his own plan to kill Jabba.<ref name="Of the Day's Annoyances: Bib Fortuna's Tale">{{StoryCite|story=Of the Day's Annoyances: Bib Fortuna's Tale|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref> [[Ree-Yees/Legends|Ree-Yees]] also plotted to murder Jabba, but he was unaware that the Hutt had a bomb planted in his body in case he tried to betray him.<ref name="Goatgrass: The Tale of Ree-Yees">{{StoryCite|story=Goatgrass: The Tale of Ree-Yees|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref> [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] was also at the palace disguised as a dancer named Arica. She was under orders by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to kill Skywalker.<ref name=":0">{{StoryCite|story=Sleight of Hand: The Tale of Mara Jade|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref>

===Battle in the rancor pit===
{{Quote|You should've bargained, Jabba. That's the last mistake you'll ever make!|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]|<ref name="RotJ" />}}
[[File:Luke-DL18.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Luke conducts "aggressive negotiations" with Jabba the Hutt.]]
After a late-night celebration, Jabba and his court were asleep when Skywalker entered the palace.<ref name="Sleight of Hand: The Tale of Mara Jade">{{StoryCite|story=Sleight of Hand: The Tale of Mara Jade|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref> After bypassing the guards with the Force, Skywalker gained access to the throne room by [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricking]] Fortuna. As Jabba awoke, Skywalker similarly attempted to influence the Hutt to release his friends with a mind trick. When this failed, Skywalker tried to assassinate the crime lord with an appropriated blaster.<ref name="RotJ" /> Jubnuk, having arrived to inform Jabba of the bad news regarding his search, witnessed this and attempted to defend Jabba.<ref name="Pop-Up Palace" /> In the scuffle that ensued, Jabba opened a trap door dropping Skywalker and Jubnuk, into the [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] pit.<ref name="RotJ" />

The rancor ate Jubnuk and continued to head for Skywalker. Skywalker found a large oblong bone and inserted it vertically into the rancor's mouth as it lifted him up. He then hid in a nook in the wall before running under the rancor and over to a metal grate door in a smaller area of the pit. He attempted to open the door, but it was closed. The area was separated by an open gate through which the rancor was now approaching. Next to the gate was a switch.<ref name="RotJ" /> Skywalker picked up the skull of a [[Bidlo Kwerve/Legends|previous victim]] and threw it at the switch.<ref name="Star Wars Customizable Card Game">[[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]</ref> The lever broke and the gate crushed the rancor's head, killing it.<ref name="RotJ" />

Enraged, Jabba sentenced Solo, Skywalker and Chewbacca to be executed by feeding them to the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] located in the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]] in the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]]. While Organa was not sentenced to death, Jabba planned to keep her as his companion.<ref name="RotJ" /> Tessek, believing Skywalker to be a true Jedi, begged the palace head of security [[Ephant Mon/Legends|Ephant Mon]] to persuade Jabba to free Skywalker. Mon visited Skywalker in the dungeons, and after sensing the Force in Skywalker, he decided it would be best to let him go. As Jabba boarded his sail barge, [[Khetanna/Legends|''Khetanna'']], Mon met with the Hutt, though he was unable to change his mind about executing the Rebels.<ref name="Old Friends: Ephant Mon's Tale">{{StoryCite|story=Old Friends: Ephant Mon's Tale|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}}</ref> Mara Jade also tried to board the sail barge in order to fulfil her mission to assassinate Skywalker, but because she had come under suspicion by the palace security detail, she was told to leave and never return.<ref name=":0" />

After the rancor's death, its body was cut up, and Jubnuk was learned to have survived his being eaten by the rancor, in large part due to a [[Tusken battle talisman|lucky talisman]] that he was tasked with finding for Jabba.<ref name="Pop-Up Palace" />

==Aftermath==
{{Main|Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon}}
{{Quote|Jabba, this is your last chance. Free us or die.|Luke Skywalker to Jabba the Hutt|<ref name="RotJ" />}}
[[File:HanLukeChewie_sentenced.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Han, Luke, and Chewie are sentenced to death.]]
Jabba and his court then traveled aboard his luxury sail barge ''Khetanna'' while the prisoners were transported by [[Bantha-II cargo skiff/Legends|cargo skiff]]. Skywalker assured Solo that he had a plan to destroy the Hutt and escape the sarlacc. They were brought to the Sarlacc's resting place, but Skywalker used his skill with the Force and a lightsaber to free both Solo and Chewbacca. With the assistance of Calrissian on the skiff, plus R2-D2 and Organa on the barge, the Rebels were able to destroy the Hutt crime lord and escape aboard a second skiff.<ref name="RotJ" /> In addition, R2-D2, as part of the Rebel's extraction plan once they rescued Solo, planted misinformation directed towards Jabba's gang that indicated that the Imperial blockade was going to descend to the planet and specifically arrest them. It worked on two fronts: It evaded the possibility of retribution from Jabba's fleet, and in addition, the massive evacuation of Jabba's criminal empire also overwhelmed the Imperial fleet, distracting the Imperials long enough for the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' and Luke's [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] to escape the Tatooine system.<ref name=":1">[[Return of the Jedi (radio)|''Return of the Jedi'' radio drama]]</ref> Parting ways when they left Tatooine, Skywalker returned to [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] with R2-D2 to fulfill his promise to Yoda and complete his training, while the others returned to the Alliance fleet, which was currently stationed near [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]].<ref name="RotJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
The siege of Jabba's Palace<ref name="TT">{{TopTrumps|set=Star Wars Episodes IV-VI|cardname=Princess Leia Organa|link=https://www.tcdb.com/ViewCard.cfm/sid/89011/cid/6360653/2004-Top-Trumps-Specials-Star-Wars-Episodes-4-6-NNO-Princess-Leia-Organa}}</ref> was an event that incorporated the beginning of the film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], which picked up where [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] left off. Although it was popularly assumed that George Lucas had thought up the opening, it had actually been the idea of writer and director [[Richard Marquand]] and [[Lawrence Kasdan]], which had eventually been revealed via transcripts of meetings included in ''[[The Making of Return of the Jedi]]''.<ref name="TMoROTJ">''[[The Making of Return of the Jedi]]''</ref>

In ''[[Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi]]'', Leia is making her offer to Jabba of Chewbacca in an attempt to gain the Hutt's trust. But things go horribly wrong. When C-3PO is knocked down by Jabba following her demand of "fifty thousand, no less," the droid's head comes off, leaving her with no means to communicate with the Hutt. With no translator and forced to reveal herself to the entire assembly, she pulls the thermal detonator and threatens to use it unless Jabba cooperates to release Solo. Lando Calrissian, noticing Boba Fett drawing his weapon, knocks him aside, sending his shot into Leia's elbow, accidentally activating the thermal detonator. As the crowd scatters, Fett takes the carbonite block of Han Solo away, and Leia and Chewbacca chase after him. Leia attempts to stop him, but is unsuccessful, one shot accidentally hitting the block's control panel and two other shots missing him. With no other option, she, Lando, and Chewbacca escape the palace shortly before it blows up when the thermal detonator goes off; C-3PO is destroyed in the explosion, and Jabba and Bib Fortuna are killed. Boba Fett brings Solo aboard the ''[[Slave I/Legends|Slave I]]'' and flees Tatooine.<ref name="Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi">''[[Star Wars Infinities: Return of the Jedi]]''</ref>
